id.description: kebab-case unique id; defaults to slug of name

flavor.description: one-line evocative summary of the weapon's look/feel/circus theme

category.description: 5e-style simple/martial split, plus 'signature': a class-defining circus weapon (e.g. Puppeteer's control wires, Juggler's flaming orbs). Signature weapons are still proficiency-gated informationally like any other, not hard-locked (see data/schema/MECHANICS.md).

type.description: locked damage-type list per DESIGN.md 'Weapons' section: physical (slashing/piercing/bludgeoning) + fire + psychic only. Fire/psychic are reserved for specific thematic weapons, not the norm.

properties.description: 5e weapon-property chassis. 'thrown' weapons should generally set canParry=false once thrown (see parryProfile); 'versatile' weapons should set versatileDamage.

versatileDamage.description: Damage die when wielded two-handed. Only meaningful if 'versatile' is present in properties.

mastery.description: Weapon Mastery property (2024-style): the default combat maneuver any proficient wielder can use with this weapon, independent of class features. Gives every class baseline tactical options (topple, cleave, push, etc.). Definitions live in data/rules/weapon-mastery.json and MASTERIES in app.js. One per weapon.

range.description: Present for thrown/ammunition weapons; omit for pure melee weapons with no thrown/ranged use.

parryProfile.description: Whether and how this weapon can parry. NOTE (v5): the per-weapon dcMod is REMOVED — a weapon no longer changes the Parry DC. The effective Parry DC is base + situational only (see MECHANICS.md §1.5). parryProfile now just says whether a weapon can parry (canParry) and against what (parryRange).

canParry.description: Whether this weapon can be used to parry at all. false for weapons that are spent/committed once used (e.g. a thrown knife after release, a loaded/fired crossbow bolt) or that are pure ranged weapons with nothing to intercept a blow with.

parryRange.description: What kind of incoming attack this weapon can parry. 'melee' = intercepts melee attacks (the common case). 'ranged' = can also deflect projectiles/ranged attacks (rare — e.g. wire-based or bladed weapons built for it). Omit if canParry is false.

note.description: Free text flavor/clarification, e.g. 'deflects projectiles', 'spent once thrown'.

proficientClasses.description: INFORMATIONAL ONLY — class ids proficient with this weapon by theme. Does NOT hard-lock who may wield it; any class may pick up any weapon. Proficiency (being in this list, per-character) governs whether proficiency bonus is added to attack rolls and to the parry effective-DC calculation, and is required to benefit from a signature weapon's special parry bonus, if any. See data/schema/MECHANICS.md.
